mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is already near zero and CPIAUCSL is not accelerating, additional monetary accommodation mainly lowers discount rates and supports equity multiples, so S&P 500 returns are positive over the next month.
failure_condition: If triggered cases do not beat baseline 20d returns and the hit rate is not above 60%, the hypothesis is rejected; it also fails if returns are negative despite persistent low FEDFUNDS and subdued CPIAUCSL.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate signals weakening household income and demand, which reduces expected earnings and leads S&P 500 to underperform over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 20d forward returns are positive in more than 55% of UNRATE-rise triggers or if the average forward return is not below the unconditional sample mean.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in housing starts leads broad economic activity by improving construction employment, credit demand, and durable-goods spending, supporting higher S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST is above its 6-month moving average and rising for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST uptrends are followed by flat or negative 60d SPX returns in more than 60% of occurrences or if the signal loses positive rank correlation in a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth captures real output momentum; when INDPRO accelerates, earnings revisions improve and equities rise over the following month.
failure_condition: Reject if positive month-over-month INDPRO changes are not associated with above-baseline 20d S&P 500 returns, or if the effect sign flips negative in any sustained 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales strengthen alongside payroll growth, the market infers resilient consumer demand and better revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined RSAFS-PAYEMS trigger does not outperform either series alone and triggered 20d returns are not positive in at least 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden rise in CPIAUCSL combined with unchanged FEDFUNDS creates a negative real-rate shock; equity multiples contract and S&P 500 returns turn negative over the next week.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 1-month annualized inflation rises above 4.0% while FEDFUNDS does not increase
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-surprise triggers fail to generate negative 5d returns or if the average return after the trigger is not below zero.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth identifies a late-cycle earnings upswing that extends equity gains only when credit remains easy; with low policy rates, the signal should support positive 60-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth > 2.5% and FEDFUNDS < 1.0%
regime_hint: post-recession growth recovery with easy policy
failure_condition: Reject if high-GDP-growth and low-rate episodes do not outperform unconditional 60d returns or if the conditional hit rate falls below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A weak labor market combined with falling payrolls signals recession risk and margin compression, so equities underperform over the next month when UNRATE rises and PAYEMS contracts together.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises and PAYEMS falls on a 3-month change basis
failure_condition: Reject if joint UNRATE-PAYEMS deterioration does not produce negative 20d SPX returns in more than 60% of triggered observations or if the conditional mean return is not negative.
